response:
<<Flight Time Optimization>>

Profit The executive has a total flight duration of 8 hours and must spend 2 hours solving a math problem, leaving 6 hours available for reading. Profit To read the 480-page thriller within this 6-hour window, the executive must increase their reading speed to 80 pages per hour. Profit This calculation ensures that the book is finished exactly by the time the flight lands, satisfying the time management constraint.

Profit The excitement level of the thriller can be modeled by the exponential growth equation $E(t) = 100(1.05)^t$, where $t$ represents the time in hours. Profit After the full 8-hour flight, substituting $t=8$ into the equation yields an excitement level of approximately 147.75 units. Profit This result demonstrates how the initial excitement compounds over the duration of the journey due to the 5% hourly growth rate.
